I posted a link saying âVitamin D & Omega-3 have a larger effect on depression than antidepressants.â
It turned out that while there is truth to the value of Vitamin D & Omega-3, the effect sizes in the article i linked were a bit cherry picked and exaggerated. When looking at larger metastudies these supplements do not have larger effect on depression than antidepressants. You should still take them, though.

I stand corrected. Instead I would like this post to serve as an inspiration of how to be wrong.

It took about 10 000 LED pixels, 200 meters of custom woven optical fibre textile (that's 112 km/70 miles of optical fibre), to create a 225 sqm breathing, immersive art museum.

I wanted to share this little Behind The Scenes video with you ð

Thank you for all the inspiration and advice I've gotten from this group. If anyone in this group is ever in Stockholm, feel free to hit me up and I'll show you around.

I wanted to share this little Behind The Scenes video with you ð

Thank you for all the inspiration and advice I've gotten from this group. If anyone in this group is ever in Stockholm, feel free to hit me up and I'll show you around @[61574989074329:2048:Andetag]

label_values.value:
This short documentary traces how ANDETAG comes to life - from the weaving of the textile to the writing of the code.
Terran followed us through the full process, capturing what we often donât show: the small decisions that shape everything.

Itâs not just a look behind the scenes - itâs an invitation to show you how we create - and why.

Watch the full film now!
Thank you @youareterran for walking with us.

#ANDETAG #BehindTheScenes #CreativeProcess #TerranFilm

label_values.label: Text
label_values.value:
What if we made advertising illegal?
This is an idea I stumbled over 15 years ago, instinctively resonated with and have grown more convinced of over the last decade.
Anyone reasonably versed in research on the topic knows how easily manipulated we humans are. To make a commercial market out of the ability to change peopleâs choices was just never a good idea. We should build a world that incentivizes _more_ agency, not less.

Is it hard to do? Sure. So letâs start with a few easy ones:
* Taxation of advertising at the point of delivery.
This means that Facebook should pay tax to Sweden for displaying ads to Swedish citizens. A flat CPM cost would be the easiest. The analytics are already there to put it in plactice.
* Prohibition of advertising in public spaces. Start with publicly owned spaces, such as the subway, then go for privately owned peoperties with public exposure. We already do this as a part of our city planning, so it also should not be a hard thing to do.

label_values.label: Text
label_values.value:
What if we made advertising illegal?
This is an idea I stumbled over 15 years ago, instinctively resonated with and have grown more convinced of over the last decade.
Anyone reasonably versed in research on the topic knows how easily manipulated we humans are. To make a commercial market out of the ability to change peopleâs choices was just never a good idea. We should build a world that incentivizes _more_ agency, not less.

Is it hard to do? Sure. So letâs start with a few easy ones:
* Taxation of advertising at the point of delivery.
This means that Facebook should pay tax to Sweden for displaying ads to Swedish citizens. A flat CPM cost would be the easiest. The analytics are already there to put it in plactice.
* Prohibition of advertising in public spaces. Start with publicly owned spaces, such as the subway, then go for privately owned peoperties with public exposure. We already do this as a part of our city planning, so it also should not be a hard thing to do.

As Kodo Simone writes:
âI think there's a world where we'll look back on our advertising-saturated era with the same bewilderment with which we now regard cigarette smoke, child labor, or public executions: a barbaric practice that we allowed to continue far too long because we couldn't imagine an alternative.â

label_values.value:
To Machine #11:
"I know youâre just a machine,
but to me youâre alive.
You are old, probably way older than I am,
but time doesn't show on you like it does on me.

Your heartbeat is strong and powerful,
beating like a drum,
echoing in my body long after we said goodbye.

When we are together, I tend to you like a mother,
always standing by your side,
making sure you have what you need.

Sometimes you get mad,
you can be really grumpy, actually,
thinking I make things too complicated.

But when we understand each other,
when you finally see my vision,
you are my best companion in creation."

A love poem to my @lindauerdornier at @ekelundweavers â¤ï¸
Yarn from @bogesunds.

#loomlove #weaving #textile #weavingmachine #textilemill #weavingmill #weavingmachine #weavingloom #dornierloom #weave #weaversofinstagram #textileprocess

label_values.value:
Before You Step Inside...

Take a moment to pause.
Whatâs the last thing that made you feel calm?
If you could step into a space created just for you, what would it feel like?

We want to hear from you - share your thoughts in the comments below. Letâs start a conversation about stillness, connection, and what it means to truly be.

label_values.label: Text
label_values.value:
Should we really use Facebook uptime as a qualifier for community inclusion?
It's happened to me twice in the last couple of weeks. First Afterglow announcing ticket sales just a few hours before the sale. Then Island of Sacred Kink just posing a signup link without any heads up. Both times, I've missed out.
Personally, I've tried to cut down on my Facebook use. I don't believe it is a platform that is healty for me (or society at large for that matter). Now I feel like I have to be on here, constantly staying up to date, or be excluded by my community.
How do we get out of this trap?

label_values.label: Text
label_values.value:
ANDRUM
The Borderland is our home. This is the soil where we grew our love, our family, and our art. It is an emotional experience to bring our work back home, to share the work that now sustains our children with the community it grew out of.

ANDRUM was the first rendition of a dream we have been growing for a while. It was a chance for us to test or vision in a space where we feel safe. 

ANDRUM is a visualisation of a deep breath. We recorded @[710302176:2048:Malin]'s breathing as she was meditating, using a mask to measure airflow. This data was the seed for the generative animations that play out through the optical fiber fabrics. The fabric itself was woven from scratch by Malin.

The installation is a continuation of Andetag, a series of 50 artworks that breath in perfect synchronisation no matter where they are placed in the world. ANDRUM was a part of this global breath, and as you were breathing with it at The Borderland, you shared breaths with people in Stockholm, in New York and Long Island.

The final version of ANDRUM will be about 4 times the size and open at a museum in Stockholm next February.

Thank you to @[635779491:2048:Cecilia Pfaff], @[505092055:2048:Charlie Caper], @[529277186:2048:Ludvig von Bahr], @[100000764191454:2048:Viktor Ansund], @[559833950:2048:Andreas HÃ¤mÃ¤lÃ¤inen], and @[514174817:2048:Oliver Vikbladh], who helped us make this happen.

If you have any photos or videos from the space, we would love to see them. It's hard to do it justice, but still nice to have see.

Thank you for breathing with us. ð«ð¨

label_values.label: Text
label_values.value:
# A word about ***giving*** advice

A lot has been written about our Advice Process and how to seek advice as you make decisions. However, not so much have been said about it actually means to ***give*** advice in such a process. So here are a few thoughts.
* Advice is a gift. As written in the *gifting* principle, "The value of a gift is unconditional. Gifting does not contemplate a return or an exchange for something of equal value." Your advice is Your gift to the person seeking advice, it is not a tool for you to get what you want.
* The objective of an advice giver is to make the advice seeker as well informed as possible. You are joining in a quest to make them understand the issue better, adding more perspectives and deeper levels of analysis. You want to help make them the best person possible to make the decision.
* Your opinion is not an advice. As you share your view, ask yourself "Does this help the advice seeker understand more about the world, or more about me?".
* Repeating something already said, or chiming in to agree or disagree, is not to provide advice.
* Arguing is not to provide advice.

To seek advice, especially on a contentious topic, is quite a cumbersome task. Our community lives on gifted time, a very scarce and precious resource. As you participate in these processes, please be mindful of how this collective resource is spent.

To seek advice, especially on a contentious topic, is quite a cumbersome task. Our community lives on gifted time, a very scarce and precious resource. As you participate in these processes, please be mindful of how that time is spent.

label_values.label: Text
label_values.value:
# A word about ***giving*** advice

A lot has been written about our Advice Process and how to seek advice as you make decisions. However, not so much have been said about it actually means to ***give*** advice in such a process. So here are a few thoughts.
* Advice is a gift. As written in the *gifting* principle, "The value of a gift is unconditional. Gifting does not contemplate a return or an exchange for something of equal value." An advice is a gift to the person seeking advice, it is not a tool for you to get what you want.
* The objective of an advice giver is to make the advice seeker as well informed as possible. You are joining in a quest to make them understand the issue better, adding more perspectives and deeper levels of analysis. You want to help make them the best person possible to make a decision.
* An advice is not the same thing as an opinion. As you share your view, ask yourself "Does this help the advice seeker understand more about the world, or more about me?".
* Repeat something already said, or chiming in to agree, is not to provide advice.
* Arguing is not to provide advice.

To seek advice, especially on a contentious topic, is quite a cumbersome task. Our community lives on gifted time, a very scarce and precious resource. As you participate in these processes, please be mindful of how that time is spent.

Andetag no 11 is my second diptych, one artwork consisting of two mirrored frames.
In the studio, we've been referring to the shape of the frame as "Havet" (the ocean). It's a 226 cm wide flow of waves and curls, a massive embrace of textile and light.

#fineart #contemporaryart #diptycg #textileart #opticalfiber #lightart #breathwork #art #breath #andetag

label_values.label: Text
label_values.value:
The most amazing news? My new series Andetag is premiering at Art Miami this year! I'm thrilled to announce that I have been picked up by Chase Contemporary - Castle Contemporary in New York, and they will be at stands #AM218 and #AM220. I'm super excited about this opportunity, and I genuinely hope to see many of you there.

Stay tuned for more updates on Andetag and feel free to reach out if you have any questions or would like to connect.

All my love, Malin

#tadaaart #fineart #art #andetag #breath #breathwork #opticalfibre #ledart #textileart #chasecontemporarynyc #chasecontemporary #artmiami

Once again inviting all you men out there to ManKind Projects New Warrior Training Adventure. Itâs a 48 hour training that have given me so much, both as a participant and as staff.
Itâs extremely well held, with 60 staff men for 35 participants, including staff who have done the same training 50 times. Itâs non-profit, with staff men even covering their own costs to be able to contribute.

This is an invitation to ask yourself what kind of man you want to be: To build integrity, hold yourself accountable and take responsibility for your own bullshit. It is not easy, but it is one of the best things I've done. It has made me a better father, husband, friend and co-worker - a better man.

The next training is September 15-17.

Please message me if you want to know more, or sign up at http://www.mkpnordic.org/nwta

Brothers,

I've been the MKPNordic Enrolment Coordinator for the last two years. This means that I'm responsible for making sure more men find their way to our NWTAs.

The most successful way this happens is when the men recently initiated call their friends and tell them about their own experience. Sure, we can create selling texts and make cool videos, but it is by you sharing your personal story, that we really reach the **right men **in the **right way.**

I encourage you to use this as an opportunity to deepen your relationship with your friend. When you go out on a limb and use your words to express what you believe he needs, what he has to gain from attending, he will feel seen and cared for. I say this from personal experience, having personally encouraged many men to attend the NWTA.

The next NWTA is at Mundekulla, Sep 15â17, and the one after is Nov 10â12 at Brevik.

Signup and more info is here: [https://mkpnordic.org/nwta](https://mkpnordic.org/nwta)

Thank you for your time,

/ Gustaf Tadaa

0709-854979

**Letâs bring out the Nordic Warrior!**

**13th MKP Nordic New Warrior Training Adventure**

**April 20th - April 23st 2022 a**t Mundekulla

**Men, New Warriors, Brothers**

On April 20th we will be gathering to initiate a group of men looking for their authentic masculine self. This will be the first move towards creating a **NWTA in a Nordic language**. All initiates are required to understand Swedish, so that some processes, or parts of processes, can be done in Swedish.

These men will come to us with their wounds, their shame and their hidden shadows. Each pair of eyes will be looking for a man they can trust. This will require an extraordinary team of Swedish and international brothers, holding this delicate new space. **Are you willing to join that team?**

I call out to all experienced **Swedish brothers** for support. We donât have many men who have done more than 2-3 trainings, so we really need your experience now.

I call out to all **international brothers** comfortable moving authentically through a multilingual space. Most of the NWTA will be in English, so you will have plenty to do. And as one of our leaders once told me: âAll men cry in the same language.â We need your experience now more than ever.

Itâs an honor for me to be given this opportunity to lead through this important transition, and I humbly ask for your support.

HÃ¶r mig!

Gustaf Tadaa

Dragon Laying Eggs

Weekend Coordinator, MKP Nordic NWTA April 2023

[guff@guff.se](mailto:guff@guff.se) / +46 709 854979

**Apply to staff latest Feb 13th at https://mkpconnect.org/civicrm/event/register?reset=1&id=55019 **(you must be logged in to MKP Connect)

**IMPORTANT DATES**
* *Staff Applications Close - Monday Feb 13 at 23:59*
* *Staff Selection and Announcement - Feb 27th*
* *First Online Staff Meeting - Tuesday March 14th 1900-2030 (CEST)*
* *Second Online Staff Meeting - Tuesday April 4th 1900-2030 (CEST)*
* *Arrival On Site - Thursday April 20th by 16:00 latest*
* *Departure From Site - Sunday April 23st at 16:30 earliest*
* *New Brother Online Celebration and Welcome Home - Thursday April 27th 1900-2030 (CEST)*

**REQUIREMENTS if selected as a Staff or MOS Man:**
* Read through, and stay up to date with all the information on the Staff site.
* Pay your staff fee and submit your medical form promptly.
* Join the communication app Slack and be responsive to issues concerning your role (answer within 48 hrs initially, 24 hours the final two weeks).
* Participate in the Online Staff Meetings (dates above)
* Carefully study the Outline (our schedule and roles sheet) and take initiative to make yourself informed of what responsibilities your roles entail.
* Connect with any assigned Staff Mentor(s) regarding your roles on the NWTA in good time.
* Following the NWTA attend a New Brother Celebration either online (see date above) or in-person (dates to be announced)
* You may be asked to mentor a New Brother after the NWTA.  That will likely involve two calls:  one by April 26 and the other about two weeks later.

I'm advising a friend setup a LED installation in his studio / party hangout. He's getting 16 ws2818 strips, with a total of 4800 pixels, and some power supplies, and I need help choosing a controller.
* It should run ArtNet and/or sACN so that it can be controlled from a computer.
* BUT - it should also be able to run some decent effects even without a computer.

I've only worked with Arduino/FastLED before, and I'm used to hacking everything myself. But my friend is no techie, so it needs something that works slightly more out of the box.
WLED is an obvious alternative... are there any good off the shelf WLED alternatives for this size setup?

Thanks!

In Swedish tradition, dating back at least a millennia, families would plant a "vÃ¥rdtrÃ¤d" in the middle of their yard as they moved to a new property. It would become the home of the "vÃ¥rd" (ward), a spirit that would protect the family from evil, and to serve as a spiritual link across generations.

The most common vÃ¥rdtrÃ¤d was the Oak.

It is said that an Oak will grow for 300 years, live for 300 years, and then die for another 300 years. In cultures across the world it has been a symbol for longevity, stability wisdom, and power. It is a pillar of the Swedish ecosystem, with over 1500 species of fungi, moss, lichen and bugs being more or less dependent on it.

**I want to plant an Oak in AlversjÃ¶ during The Borderland.**

I'm not an arborist or an experienced gardener, so I need all the advice and help I can get. We got to plan logistics, get digging, decorating the space, creating rituals, chanting, blessing, etc. etc.

Of course, we also need people who are decently local, to help with occasional caretaking throughout the year.

â*A society grows great when old men plant trees in whose shade they shall never sit.*â â Greek Proverb.

If you're interested, please join this WhatsApp group: https://chat.whatsapp.com/GiJAEN4hyVHEs4eWKXtvOs

I want to give back to all of those who have supported me throughout this winter.

Starting today, everyone who owns an NFT can log on to www.imbuedart.com to claim a free high quality signed print of their NFT.

The photos are printed in 70x70cm (Love) or 50x50cm (Awe) on fine art paper and framed in a black 20Ã30mm wooden frame, with anti-reflective art glass.
A signed proof of authenticity is attached to back of each print.

A photo locked up in an app is not the art I want to create.
I want you to experience it as a part of your world.

#imbued #fineart #nft #photo #printondemand #1of1

label_values.value:
One week ago, I sold Imbued with Awe to a collector. This is the third piece of art Iâve ever sold, and the most expensive, so Iâm celebrating this week!

So, whatâs going on with the rest of the Imbued collection?

To be honest, the process with Imbued so far has been a bit of a struggle. Itâs hard to mix the creative process, with travelling and exhibiting. So, instead of producing one artwork at a time and exhibiting one by one, I will create the remaining 5 artworks over the next 4 months and then release them all at the same time.

So expect a bit of silence now, and Iâll be back with a BANG next fall!

label_values.value:
This is @[550206780:2048:Daniel] and I, doing one of the very first shifts ever for The Borderland Clown Police, the year it was created: 2013. Noses, batons, helmet, soap bubble gun. Ready for duty.

The Clown Police is the core of our safety work at The Borderland.

The idea of a Clown Police came from the 2002 live role playing game Futuredrome, where the group was tasked by the artificial intelligence controlling the city to "Arrest anyone not having fun enough".

We liked the idea of approaching the issue of safety, not just as mitigation ("let's not have bad things happen"), but as promoting something, of spreading joy! ("let's have good things happen!").

Over 7 years, over 1000 people have "clowned" at The Borderland. I still do it every year, and it's something that really makes me connect with the land and the community. It makes me feel a strong sense of belonging and ownership. I warmly recommend it, especially for newbies, since it's such a fast route into understanding the event and the culture.

There are still plenty of shifts that needs to be taken, so please do a service to yourself and to our community and sign up here

https://coda.io/d/Clown-police-2022_dN7sWwrsLHW/Welcome-to_suUux

Check above link to ***coda **Clown Police 2022* and follow the instructions upon **SIGN UP button**.

label_values.value:
After 20 years as an entrepreneur, startup advisor, entrepreneurship evangelist and motivational speaker, this is one of the most important takes Iâve discovered: Entrepreneurship does not lead to social mobility.
The startup scene is the outsourcing of corporate R&D, moving the risk of innovation from capital to people, having young people work for noodles, so that those with money can reap the rewards.

However, what this post it is missing is that failure is also experience. Starting a business is one of the best ways to learn about yourself and the world around youâ¦ But if you think you should do a startup to get rich, thatâs a bad bet.

Yes, you might get lucky. I have been lucky, and Iâm deeply thankful for that. Iâm also very aware of the fact that financial security provided by my parents allowed me to do my second and third try. The point is: the odds are not in your favor.

It's also FUCKING HUGE.

I belong to the 2-3% of people with aphantasia, meaning I am unable to visualize imagery, I have no âmindâs eyeâ.
Discovering this was mind blowing. To suddenly realize that other people actually see things with their eyes closed!!!
Research on this is still young, as it was only scientifically outlined in 2005. Here is a podcast episode describing the phenomena and the current research on the topic.

Test yourself: https://aphantasia.com/vviq/

https://open.spotify.com/episode/097YA18Uk43deUzoreZpSQ?si=gsd3MYbhQIO7vMejqx0PAA

label_values.value:
WELCOME HOME! THE BORDERLAND HAS BOUGHT 99HA OF LAND IN ALVERSJÃ!!!

The crazy vision of having community land for the Borderland folk is coming through! The unbelievable dream is becoming reality! ðð«ð§¡ð

We're still in disbelief but today at 16:00 / 4pm we signed the papers to the AlversjÃ¶ property and paid the down payment of 1.18 MSEK (10%) ð²ð The bidding was slow and nerve wrecking. The final price was 11.8 MSEK ð - a lot less than anticipated.

The total sum will be paid in a combination of donations, community loans and a bank loan with good terms. We hope to get access to the land by June this year âºï¸

It's time for our dreams!

So many of us have been part of this dream, supporting it in so many ways: donating memberships, financial gifts, organizing auctions, going on land scouting trips, taking part in BL.L live streams, offering advice on Talk, engaging in discussions on Facebook and Discord, pledging loans, organizing ceremonial gatherings to share good wishes for the project, offering healthy critique to help us improve, and most important: dreaming about what the land could become!

We are so so so excited to say out loud that AlversjÃ¶ now can welcome our dreams ð§¡ð 
If you have a dream, you can add it to the land dream generator:

https://dreams.theborderland.se/dream-generator

When can we access the land and how?

We will hopefully achieve ownership of AlversjÃ¶ in June. If you want to visit the land before we actually own it, you can use the Swedish Freedom to Roam Act which allows you to visit for a walk and a swim. You have the right to do so with respect, such as not impacting the crops when walking on agricultural fields.

See more here:

https://visitsweden.com/what-to-do/nature-outdoors/nature/sustainable-and-rural-tourism/about-the-right-of-public-access/

Please also remember that fields are leased to farmers this year and we want to sustain a good relationship with them.

Borderland 2022

To host the Borderland in AlversjÃ¶ we need to negotiate with local farmers who still have the right to farm the land 2022. However, we hope this process will be smooth and constructive.  We will know for certain say mid March. IF, for some reason, Borderland cannot happen on AlversjÃ¶, we have a backup plan on a different site.

Curious to know more about AlversjÃ¶?

For context, links to pictures of the land, description and maps, check out the previous AP on bidding on AlversjÃ¶ and hosting Borderland event there:

WELCOME HOME! THE BORDERLAND HAS BOUGHT 99HA OF LAND IN ALVERSJÃ!!!

The crazy vision of having community land for the Borderland folk is coming through! The unbelievable dream is becoming reality! ðð«ð§¡ð

We're still in disbelief but today at 16:00 / 4pm we signed the papers to the AlversjÃ¶ property and paid the down payment of 1.18 MSEK (10%) ð²ð The bidding was slow and nerve wrecking. The final price was 11.8 MSEK ð - a lot less than anticipated.

The total sum will be paid in a combination of donations, community loans and a bank loan with good terms. We hope to get access to the land by June this year âºï¸

It's time for our dreams!

So many of us have been part of this dream, supporting it in so many ways: donating memberships, financial gifts, organizing auctions, going on land scouting trips, taking part in BL.L live streams, offering advice on Talk, engaging in discussions on Facebook and Discord, pledging loans, organizing ceremonial gatherings to share good wishes for the project, offering healthy critique to help us improve, and most important: dreaming about what the land could become!

We are so so so excited to say out loud that AlversjÃ¶ now can welcome our dreams ð§¡ð 
If you have a dream, you can add it to the land dream generator:

https://dreams.theborderland.se/dream-generator

When can we access the land and how?

We will hopefully achieve ownership of AlversjÃ¶ in June. If you want to visit the land before we actually own it, you can use the Swedish Freedom to Roam Act which allows you to visit for a walk and a swim. You have the right to do so with respect, such as not impacting the crops when walking on agricultural fields.

See more here:

https://visitsweden.com/what-to-do/nature-outdoors/nature/sustainable-and-rural-tourism/about-the-right-of-public-access/

Please also remember that fields are leased to farmers this year and we want to sustain a good relationship with them.

Borderland 2022

To host the Borderland in AlversjÃ¶ we need to negotiate with local farmers who still have the right to farm the land 2022. However, we hope this process will be smooth and constructive.  We will know for certain say mid March. IF, for some reason, Borderland cannot happen on AlversjÃ¶, we have a backup plan on a different site thanks to Jana and the scouting crew and Ylva, who also talked to a landowner that might be open to us renting a piece of land on his property. Once we know how the conversations with the AlversjÃ¶ farmers ends in about two weeks, we will be able to fully open our sails âµ

What needs to be decided first is of course the preliminary dates! WHEN would we like the Borderland to happen? Piotr will start an Advice Process on this in the coming days.

label_values.label: Text
label_values.value:
Putin: âCheck out the military MIGHT of Russia!!!â
US/EU: âWell what about our TOOTHPICK?â
The so called âsanctionsâ are an embarrassment to the western world. Theyâre are more or less an endorsement of the Russian invasion. They are a Chamberlainian âPeace in our timeâ-response to an all-out invation of a sovereign European state.

Any political or economic response has to be proportional to the military force exerted by Russia.

* Freeze all foreign assets of all Russian companies.
* Block imports, block exports completely. (No, Italy, you may not have exceptions for Lamborghini and Gucci ð¤¦ð»ââï¸)
* Make any type of trade with Russia a crime, and enforce the same sanctions to any international company who breaks the embargo.
* Extradite, close borders and freeze all assets of all Russian citizens associated to the government or major Russian corporations.

Yes, it will cost, but the alternative will cost us more.

Sanctions has to be felt by the Russian people the same way that the bombs over Kyiv are felt by the Ukrainians. It has to inflict a complete collapse of Russian society.
Putin needs to be overthrown, or this is just the beginning. His actions are not driven by logic or calculated strategy. He is an old man, afraid of his own mortality, seeking to make himself immortal as the ultimate savior of the Russian empire.

He will stop at nothing.

* Block imports, block exports completely. (No, Italy, you may not have exceptions for Lamborghini and Gucci ð¤¦ð»ââï¸)
* Make any type of trade with Russia a crime.
* Extradite, close borders and freeze all assets of all Russian citizens  associated to the government or major Russian corporations.

Yes, it will cost, but the alternative will cost us more.

Sanctions has to be felt by the Russian people the same way that the bombs over Kyiv are felt by the Ukrainians. It has to inflict a complete collapse of Russian society.
Putin needs to be overthrown, or this is just the beginning. His actions are not driven by logic or calculated strategy. He is an old man, afraid of his own mortality, seeking to make himself immortal as the ultimate savior of the Russian empire.

He will stop at nothing.

This is an Imbued with Awe NFT Weave.
Each NFT contains its own high-resolution photograph, with an unique color configuration. It invites its owner to a Ritual of Awe, where they get to Imbue sacred words onto the blockchain.

Do You own an Imbued with Love NFT?
If so, you have an exclusive opportunity to mint your own Awe NFT at a discounted price, before we open to the public.
Checkout http://www.imbuedart.com/mint-nft for more info.

label_values.label: Text
label_values.value:
My work is my meditation, and Imbued with Awe came out of the dedication to that meditation. I believe the experience of Awe comes from within ourselves, and that through practice, we all have the ability to create it.

This is Imbued with Awe, the second in a series of seven Imbued artworks. Imbued with Awe represents the religious use of textiles, the Swedish âÃkdynaâ, the pillow you place in the wagon to carry you to church. It is an invitation to journey towards that connection with something bigger than yourself.

It is being exhibited at Zona Maco, Latin Americaâs largest arts fair, Feb 9-13 in Mexico City.

#imbuedwithawe #artwork #art #fineart #ledart #digitalart #nftart #ledart #artoftheday #cryptoartist #modernart #artgallery #nft #cryptoart #nftcollector

label_values.label: Text
label_values.value:
My first ever art auction starts now! 
During my years as an artist I have made several big installations and traveled around the world with them. 
But I never had a piece auctioned out and actually sold to a single buyer. Until now! â¦I hope.
Itâs vulnerable to put my art out there in this new way and Iâm both super exited for the coming week and scared that all our work will be in vain. Imbued with Love is both a deeply personal expression and a technological experiment. Optical fibre textiles, LEDs, NFTs, all entangled in my own relationship with Love. Will people connect with it the same way I do?

Read more about Imbued with Love and follow the auction in real-time, for the next 6 days, on my web page.
http://www.imbuedart.com/artwork/love

label_values.label: Text
label_values.value:
"Nice fabric. How much per meter?"
In relation to NFTs, many people say "Why can't i just right click and choose Save As...?"
But it's not only the NFT world that has "right-clickers". Working with textiles means I often have to to deal with people believing that my art is something that should sold as a fungible commodity. Despite being one of the oldest artforms, textile art stays undervalued, mainly because it is still considered to be "womenâs work".
That's why I stopped calling myself "textile artist". I realized I wanted to be an artist in my own right. What materials, skills or technology I is are secondary to the concept and the artistic vision. I'm an artist. This is my art.
#fineart #nonfungible #textileart

label_values.label: Text
label_values.value:
My earliest memories are those of touching textiles. The tactile aspect have always been central to my art. My exhibitions have even had signs saying âPlease touch the art gentlyâ, not to tell people to be gentle, but to get them to understand that they are _encouraged_ to touch.
Weaving light in to the art have allowed me to explore the tactile aspects of the digital world. How can we use our new tools to connect, to create interaction, community?

This is a part of my exploration of using NFTs in Imbued with Love. It allows people across time and space to share a love story, and to have that story he expressed in physical form.

#love #artworld #fineart #ledart #nftcreative #imbuedartworks #trippyart #imbued #interactiveart #smarttextiles #digitalart #artworld #imbuedartworks #artoftheday #raredigitalart

label_values.label: Text
label_values.value:
In 1 hour (Friday 22.00 CET), we drop the last 50 the Imbued with Love NFT Weaves at www.imbuedart.com.

What does it mean to buy one of these?

Imbue your NFT
As the caretaker of an NFT Weave, you are invited to a ritual where you put their interpretation of art into words. These are stored permanently on the blockchain and are used to create the color and light that flows through the physical artwork itself, somewhere in the world.

Show off your art
As a Weave caretaker, you will get access to high resolution photo and video material of the artwork that is unique to your NFT. These are made for you to display on a digital photo frame or screen in your home. You could consider this to be similar to owning a signed limited edition copy of an artwork. It's not the same as owning the real thing, but it's also not the same as buying a mass produced poster. 

It's just the beginning
An NFT forms permanent relationship between the artist and her community. It's an opportunity for patrons and fans to invest in art they love and be able to get something back. As the project goes on, we will provide additional perks and opportunities for our Weave caretakers. A few surprises can be expected.

label_values.label: Text
label_values.value:
Weâre doing or first NFT drop!
This is Imbued with Love. It is a 110x90cm wall hanging artwork made from optical fiber textile. It is connected to 99 NFT Weaves, digital tokens that allow their owners to go through a ritual and Imbue the artwork with their own interpretation of love. Each new owner gets to charge the art with even more intention and devotion.
These words are stored on the blockchain forever. The artwork itself connects via Wi-Fi and uses these words of Love to generate the patterns of light and color you see in this video.
What you see when you look at this artwork is the loving words of hundreds, eventually thousands of people, expressed through trough technology. The threads of the weave, becoming tokens of love.
On Friday at 18.00 CET , we release the first 40 NFTs at www.imbuedart.com. Imbued with Love will be auctioned out starting Dec 27.

label_values.label: Text
label_values.value:
I love my wife.
One late night last spring, we sat around talking about this weird NFT-phenomena. This booming new market for digital art. People selling GIFs! For millions!
As I was trying to explain the technology behind this to her, an idea started to grow... Could this be connected with the physical world? What if the textiles she was working on could be connected to the internet and interact with the blockchain? We kept talking for a while, then let it go, and went to bed.
The next morning I kept thinking about it, and I actually picked up the phone to call my crypto expert friend Christian Ander of bt.cx to better understand wtf this was all about. As I got home, it turned out the idea had stuck even more in Malin's head. She had spent the entire day researching the subject, listening to podcasts, reading articles, and even started writing a grant application for a project - Non Fungible Textile.

Since the project kicked off this August, she has worked her ass off. I've been doing my best with babysitting, project support, emotional support... and a little bit of coding.

And now, it is real. The photo you see here is a small snapshot of "Imbued with Love", the first of seven artworks of "Imbued - Thread to token". This is the world's first Hybrid NFT Art. Custom woven optical fibre textile artworks, that connect via wifi to the blockchain. The owner of the artwork, as well as the 99 owners of its interconnected NFT Weaves, will have the power to share their own memories of love, to Imbue the artwork with their own love. These stories are eternally stored in the blockchain and used by the physical artwork to generate a light pattern that will flow through its fibers. Art, imbued with memories.

The website is finally live at www.imbuedart.com.

The first NFTs are going on sale Nov 26 and the first artwork, Imbued with Love going up for auction on Dec 27.

I also want to send some love to Viktor Ansund, Rikard Hjort, Amanda MungsgÃ¥rd, Annelie NÃ¤s, Daniel Daboczy, Bear Kittay, Ola Ahlvarsson and all the people who have supported the idea one way or another. You are all with us on the journey. This is just getting started!

#love #nft #nonfungibletokens #artworld #opticalfibers #cryptoart #nftcollector #nftcommunity #nftcreative #imbuedartworks #nftart  #crypto

These stories are eternally stored in the blockchain and used by the physical artwork to generate a light pattern that will flow through its fibers. Art, imbued with memories.

The website is finally live at www.imbuedart.com.

The first NFTs are going on sale Nov 26 and the first artwork, Imbued with Love going up for auction on Dec 27.

I love my wife.
One late night last spring, we sat around talking about this weird NFT-phenomena. This booming new market for digital art. People selling GIFs! For millions!
As I was trying to explain the technology behind this to her, an idea started to grow... Could this be connected with the physical world? What if the textiles she was working on could be connected to the internet and interact with the blockchain? We kept talking for a while, then let it go, and went to bed.
The next morning I kept thinking about it, and I actually picked up the phone to call my crypto expert friend Christian Ander of bt.cx to better understand wtf this was all about. As I got home, it turned out the idea had stuck even more in Malin's head. She had spent the entire day researching the subject, listening to podcasts, reading articles, and even started writing a grant application for a project - Non Fungible Textile.

Since the project kicked off this August, she has worked her ass off. I've been doing my best with babysitting, project support, emotional support... and a little bit of coding.

And now, it is real. The photo you see here is a small snapshot of "Imbued with Love", the first of seven artworks of "Imbued - Thread to token". This is the Word's First Hybrid NFT Art. Custom woven optical fibre textile artworks, that connect via wifi to the blockchain. The owner of the artwork, as well as the 99 owners of its interconnected NFT Weaves, will have the power to share their own memories of love, to Imbue the artwork with their own love. These stories are eternally stored in the blockchain and used by the physical artwork to generate a light pattern that will flow through its fibers. Art, imbued with memories.

The website is finally live at www.imbuedart.com.

The first NFTs are going on sale Nov 26 and the first artwork, Imbued with Love going up for auction on Dec 27.

I also want to send some love to Viktor Ansund, Rikard Hjort, Amanda MungsgÃ¥rd, Annelie NÃ¤s, Daniel Daboczy, Bear Kittay, Ola Ahlvarsson and all the people who have supported the idea one way or another. You are all with us on the journey. This is just getting started!

Come with us to Mexico!
For three months, from Dec 1 to Feb 28, @[710302176:2048:Malin], Frej, Iris and I will move to San Jose del Cabo. We'll work on her new exciting NFT textile artworks (launching soon), join the local, bubbling burner community at Crania, hang out with @[4301491:2048:Bear], @[3616855:2048:Katiyana] and Kuma at @[272171986216792:274:Hotel El Ganzo], join the @[332269353556235:274:Mayan Warrior] for an epic new year, see whales and dolphins, and play in the sun with our kids.

Come join us! For a week, a month, or maybe three? Need to work? We're working from here too! Have kids? Bring em! Don't have kids? You can borrow ours! (...or just come to play with adults).
We would love to have more company, and will help out finding great places to stay, play or work at.

(Attached: the a photo of our penthouse apartment)

Come with us to Mexico!
For three months, from Dec 1 to Feb 28, @[710302176:2048:Malin], Frej, Iris and I will move to San Jose del Cabo,. We'll work on her new exciting NFT textile artworks (launching soon), join the local, bubbling burner community at Crania, hang out with @[4301491:2048:Bear], @[3616855:2048:Katiyana] and Kuma at @[272171986216792:274:Hotel El Ganzo], join the @[332269353556235:274:Mayan Warrior] for an epic new year, see whales and dolphins, and play in the sun with our kids.

Come join us! For a week, a month, or maybe three? Have kids? Bring em! Don't have kids? You can borrow ours! (...or just come to play with adults).
We would love to have more company, and will help out finding great places to stay, play or work at.

(Attached: the a photo of our penthouse apartment)

Looking for two memberships for The Crossing (of the Borderland). Any leads?

Edit: weâre set. Edit 2: Looking for MORE! Edit 3: Got em!

label_values.label: Text
label_values.value:
This post is a continuation of the conversation that @[1190304174:2048:Emil Steen Ravbjerg] started about **Radical Inclusion**. After spending quite some time putting together a small essay on the topic, I felt this was better suited as its own post.

I find Radical Inclusion to be the most important principle we have, and the one that Iâve had to be the most vigilant about standing up for. It is the principle that makes us a community and not a club. Itâs what keeps us at arms length from being a cult. I want to first share my philosophical view of the principle, then give some concrete examples from the last decade of community events.

*First of all, it is important to remember this fundamental fact: **inclusion sucks**.*

It is uncomfortable to include. It's a basic human instinct to fear the stranger and stay within the tribe. To put up borders and separate Us from Them. We all have different boundaries for this discomfort, but itâs important to recognise this human need that exists within all of us.

Second, the way I like to think about the word â**radical**â, is as âtaken as far as possibleâ. That is, we can never truly BE radical, all we can do is continuously push ourselves to reach even further. Being radical is a never-ending strife. Being radical is a process.

This means that Radical Inclusion is: ***âto constantly push yourself to be even more inclusiveâ***. It means to always be uncomfortable because you are including more people, more aspects of yourself, more expressions, more opinions, and more diversity than you are actually comfortable with.

***If youâre comfortable, youâre not practicing radical inclusion. Inclusion sucks.***

Itâs also important to notice that the 10 principles are often in tension with each other. Someoneâs Radical Self-Expression, could be in conflict with the inclusion of other expressions. The demand for Radical Self-Reliance can prevent some from participation. The principles are a framework for conversation and negotiation, and there is no correct way of interpreting and weighing them. I am writing this, not because I want to tell people what to do, but because I hope to enrich the conversation.

â

One way that Radical Inclusion is put through the test is because of **scarcity**.

In 2014, the Borderland sold out for the first time, at 300 memberships. At this point, I knew that we would sell out for every year forward. What had been âeveryone is welcomeâ, had to have a system for inclusion because of scarcity. My line was, and always will be, what is now written in to the bylaws: *âEveryone, to the largest possible extent, should have an equal opportunity to attend.â* Pretty much immediately, the first voices were raised for putting up borders for the borderland. 

These calls could be divided in two groups:
1. Certain responsibilities require certain people, so we should choose people based on skills and predicted contribution.
1. Me and my friends have been a part of building this community, so we have a right to âstayâ.

The first one has repeatedly turned out to be bullshit. We have loads of talented people who want to attend, and we have never ever run out of talent. We tend to overvalue experience, and underestimate the passionate force of the newcomer. When we scratch the surface, we often find that this first argument stems from motivated reasoning for the second argument.

The second argument is a tougher one to handle. As you approach the border of the Borderland, someone will walk up to you, give you a hug and say: âWelcome homeâ. But if this is âhomeâ, then who are we to tell you that you are not allowed to come home? For many people, who have truly found a new life and a new home within this community, to say: âyou canât comeâ is equal to religious excommunication or banishment from the tribe. From an instinctive perspective, being excluded from the tribe is equal to death.

The claim to incumbency is not to be taken lightly. **Belonging is core to community.**

I was the one who proposed and developed the pretty hard-line lottery system weâve had up until today. That system requires each and one of to come to terms with the potential of being excluded. It has been stressful for many, and the system is not without drawbacks. But with this, we have managed to build the event up to 3200 people and yet only have the 7 people on the board (and their +1âs) be above the principle of âequal opportunityâ through the lottery. This idea stands in stark contrast to Burning Man, where almost half of the tickets are distributed top down (around 35k / 80k, if I remember correctly). The system has been discussed at depth elsewhere, so I will not go further into that debate here.

â

Another way to think about inclusion is to consider **access to information**. Whatâs the point of everyone being welcome to an event, if they donât know it exists? With The Borderland, weâve been trying our best to fly under the radar, to turn down journalists (without memberships), and ask not to be written about. Can we really tell ourselves that we are inclusive, and yet try to stay a secret? How broadly do we need to announce our existence for us to practice Radical Inclusion?

In 2016, I took the initiative to start up Urban Burn, a 700 people, 5 days, indoor festival in Stockholm. Here, I wanted to interpret radical inclusion differently, and extend it further. First of all, the âradical self-relianceâ of an outdoor festival (be it in a quarry or a desert), is in itself a boundary to inclusion. Putting it inside the city, having people sleep in their own homes, made it less scary and inaccessible.

**But who should be invited?** For the Borderland, we had a strict policy to not do any marketing, to avoid the press and only grow through word of mouth. In a way, thatâs not very inclusive. Just look around, the reason our community has an overrepresentation of a certain skin color is not because of active exclusion, but of failure to reach out and choose inclusion. For Urban Burn, we decided to start an Outreach group, who would actively contact communities and individuals who we thought would appreciate the event.

We also paid *gasp* for Facebook ads *GASP*. Here, in the tension between radical inclusion and deco modification, we swallow our pride and make the decision: if we are to radically include, we need to find ways to reach outside our bubble. Maybe this requires marketing?

â

I have two more recent events that I want to mention in the context of Radical Inclusion, that challenge what I think about inclusion in different ways.

The first one is **Morph**, who have made the decision to actively exclude people who are accused of consent violations. That is, no âtrialâ happens, but accusations are taken at face value and those accused are excluded without getting to defend themselves, know the accusation, who accused them, or have their experience included at all.

As I interpret the intention of this process, it is to be able to include those who otherwise would feel excluded because of the inclusion of the banned individuals. It is to allow those who are afraid of repercussions to find the courage and support to speak up. It is to radically include the âspeakersâ by radically excluding the reported.

From this perspective, this is indeed pushing the limits of what we mean by inclusion. Itâs doing this through a process that is very uncomfortable, both for those banned, and Iâm sure for those who are making the decisions. To me, **this is definitely Radical Inclusion.**

My objection against what Morph is doing is against the lack of transparency and accountability. **Secrecy breeds dishonesty.** Lack of accountability breeds abuse. While the specific accusers are protected short-term by this process, I do not believe it contributes to a world where accusers in general are more likely to be listened to. Secret anonymous accusations and secret autocratic decisions erode trust within a community. I believe this is actually long-term counterproductive to the work we try to do on consent.

Speaking of community, the second aspect is a question that is yet unanswered: âHow will Morph take responsibility for those excluded?â. This is something that makes us âa communityâ. In a community, you know that those judged for wrongdoing will still be there. There is no such thing as just exclusion, you have to handle their long term presence. Even if you go as far as put them in jail, you have to take responsibility for running that jail. You canât just say âGTFOâ, and think the story is over. A community takes responsibility for those who donât live up to the standards of the community, and **support them on a path back to redemption**. If not, youâre not a community, youâre a club.

â

The second current event that comes into mind is the â**All-Inclusive (and probably not a) burn**â. This event used a system of exclusion that has been fairly common throughout the years. The system for sign-up was first-come-first-serve. Instead, the information about the event was selectively spread, so that those who were closer to the organisers had time to sign up before more public posts were made. Although this can be seen as unfair, it is pretty natural for people who spend time initiating a completely new event to want their friends to be able to come. After all, thatâs probably the reason they started the event in the first place. 

These kinds of events are not âcommunity eventsâ. Rather, they are â**events within the community**â, smaller gatherings of friends that happen every other weekend across the nordics. These events feed energy and passion into the community, but they are not the community. They are not âburnsâ. At least âAll-Inclusiveâ have the honesty to admit that they (probably) arenât. 

â

**Radical Inclusion** is maybe the most challenging of all of the 10 principles. It is **uncomfortable** (and should be uncomfortable) for each of us, and for the community as a whole. It is a challenge that reverberates through society, in conversations about equality, immigration, democracy and more. **By the hand of globalisation, it is truly a challenge of our time.** While I here try to look at systems and processes, inclusion is something deeply personal. We canât do it without starting with ourselves: with our own emotions, with our own home, our group of friends and the communities we live in.

So finally, I want to recognise the fact **I speak about inclusion from a point of privilege**. In fact, I believe that any voice speaking about the inclusion of someone other than themselves is a privileged voice. I want to ask all of you, as you speak about this topic, as you make decisions about inclusion, to recognise this privilege. **Your voice has the power to take away what some might consider their âhomeâ**, their very sense of belonging. I ask you to show humility, to show compassion.

I spent two weeks writing this text, and I have no intention of going into Facebook-debate-mode over it. If you want to start a thread on Talk, please invite me. If you want to contact me directly, please go ahead. I will personally not participate in the comments section of this post.

Thank you for reading,

/ Gustaf

This post is a continuation of the conversation that @[1190304174:2048:Emil Steen Ravbjerg] started about **Radical Inclusion**. After spending quite some time putting together a small essay on the topic, I felt this was better suited as its own post.

I find Radical Inclusion to be the most important principle we have, and the one that Iâve had to be the most vigilant about standing up for. It is the principle that makes us a community and not a club. Itâs what keeps us at arms length from being a cult. I want to first share my philosophical view of the principle, then give some concrete examples from the last decade of community events.

*First of all, it is important to remember this fundamental fact: **inclusion sucks**.*

It is uncomfortable to include. It's a basic human instinct to fear the stranger and stay within the tribe. To put up borders and separate Us from Them. We all have different boundaries for this discomfort, but itâs important to recognise this human need that exists within all of us.

Second, the way I like to think about the word â**radical**â, is as âtaken as far as possibleâ. That is, we can never truly BE radical, all we can do is continuously push ourselves to reach even further. Being radical is a never-ending strife. Being radical is a process.

This means that Radical Inclusion is: ***âto constantly push yourself to be even more inclusiveâ***. It means to always be uncomfortable because you are including more people, more aspects of yourself, more expressions, more opinions, and more diversity than you are actually comfortable with.

***If youâre comfortable, youâre not practicing radical inclusion. Inclusion sucks.***

Itâs also important to notice that the 10 principles are often in tension with each other. Someoneâs Radical Self-Expression, could be in conflict with the inclusion of other expressions. The demand for Radical Self-Reliance can prevent some from participation. The principles are a framework for conversation and negotiation, and there is no correct way of interpreting and weighing them. I am writing this, not because I want to tell people what to do, but because I hope to enrich the conversation.

â

One way that Radical Inclusion is put through the test is because of **scarcity**.

In 2014, the Borderland sold out for the first time, at 300 memberships. At this point, I knew that we would sell out for every year forward. What had been âeveryone is welcomeâ, had to have a system for inclusion because of scarcity. My line was, and always will be, what is now written in to the bylaws: *âEveryone, to the largest possible extent, should have an equal opportunity to attend.â* Pretty much immediately, the first voices were raised for putting up borders for the borderland. 

These calls could be divided in two groups:
1. Certain responsibilities require certain people, so we should choose people based on skills and predicted contribution.
1. Me and my friends have been a part of building this community, so we have a right to âstayâ.

The first one has repeatedly turned out to be bullshit. We have loads of talented people who want to attend, and we have never ever run out of talent. We tend to overvalue experience, and underestimate the passionate force of the newcomer. When we scratch the surface, we often find that this first argument stems from motivated reasoning for the second argument.

The second argument is a tougher one to handle. As you approach the border of the Borderland, someone will walk up to you, give you a hug and say: âWelcome homeâ. But if this is âhomeâ, then who are we to tell you that you are not allowed to come home? For many people, who have truly found a new life and a new home within this community, to say: âyou canât comeâ is equal to religious excommunication or banishment from the tribe. From an instinctive perspective, being excluded from the tribe is equal to death.

The claim to incumbency is not to be taken lightly. **Belonging is core to community.**

I was the one who proposed and developed the pretty hard-line lottery system weâve had up until today. That system requires each and one of to come to terms with the potential of being excluded. It has been stressful for many, and the system is not without drawbacks. But with this, we have managed to build the event up to 3200 people and yet only have the 7 people on the board (and their +1âs) be above the principle of âequal opportunityâ through the lottery. This idea stands in stark contrast to Burning Man, where almost half of the tickets are distributed top down (around 35k / 80k, if I remember correctly). The system has been discussed at depth elsewhere, so I will not go further into that debate here.

â

Another way to think about inclusion is to consider **access to information**. Whatâs the point of everyone being welcome to an event, if they donât know it exists? With The Borderland, weâve been trying our best to fly under the radar, to turn down journalists (without memberships), and ask not to be written about. Can we really tell ourselves that we are inclusive, and yet try to stay a secret? How broadly do we need to announce our existence for us to practice Radical Inclusion?

In 2016, I took the initiative to start up Urban Burn, a 700 people, 5 days, indoor festival in Stockholm. Here, I wanted to interpret radical inclusion differently, and extend it further. First of all, the âradical self-relianceâ of an outdoor festival (be it in a quarry or a desert), is in itself a boundary to inclusion. Putting it inside the city, having people sleep in their own homes, made it less scary and inaccessible.

**But who should be invited?** For the Borderland, we had a strict policy to not do any marketing, to avoid the press and only grow through word of mouth. In a way, thatâs not very inclusive. For Urban Burn, we decided to start an Outreach group, who would actively contact communities and individuals who we thought would appreciate the event.

We also paid *gasp* for Facebook ads *GASP*. Here, in the tension between radical inclusion and deco modification, we swallow our pride and make the decision: if we are to radically include, we need to find ways to reach outside our bubble. Maybe this requires marketing?

â

I have two more recent events that I want to mention in the context of Radical Inclusion, that challenge what I think about inclusion in different ways.

The first one is **Morph**, who have made the decision to actively exclude people who are accused of consent violations. That is, no âtrialâ happens, but accusations are taken at face value and those accused are excluded without getting to defend themselves, know the accusation, who accused them, or have their experience included at all.

As I interpret the intention of this process, it is to be able to include those who otherwise would feel excluded because of the inclusion of the banned individuals. It is to allow those who are afraid of repercussions to find the courage and support to speak up. It is to radically include the âspeakersâ by radically excluding the reported.

From this perspective, this is indeed pushing the limits of what we mean by inclusion. Itâs doing this through a process that is very uncomfortable, both for those banned, and Iâm sure for those who are making the decisions. To me, **this is definitely Radical Inclusion.**

My objection against what Morph is doing is against the lack of transparency and accountability. **Secrecy breeds dishonesty.** Lack of accountability breeds abuse. While the specific accusers are protected short-term by this process, I do not believe it contributes to a world where accusers in general are more likely to be listened to. Secret anonymous accusations and secret autocratic decisions erode trust within a community. I believe this is actually long-term counterproductive to the work we try to do on consent.

Speaking of community, the second aspect is a question that is yet unanswered: âHow will Morph take responsibility for those excluded?â. This is something that makes us âa communityâ. In a community, you know that those judged for wrongdoing will still be there. There is no such thing as just exclusion, you have to handle their long term presence. Even if you go as far as put them in jail, you have to take responsibility for running that jail. You canât just say âGTFOâ, and think the story is over. A community takes responsibility for those who donât live up to the standards of the community, and **support them on a path back to redemption**. If not, youâre not a community, youâre a club, or even worse: a cult.

â

The second current event that comes into mind is the â**All-Inclusive (and probably not a) burn**â. This event used a system of exclusion that has been fairly common throughout the years. The system for sign-up was first-come-first-serve. Instead, the information about the event was selectively spread, so that those who were closer to the organisers had time to sign up before more public posts were made. Although this can be seen as unfair, it is pretty natural for people who spend time initiating a completely new event to want their friends to be able to come. After all, thatâs probably the reason they started the event in the first place. 

These kinds of events are not âcommunity eventsâ. Rather, they are â**events within the community**â, smaller gatherings of friends that happen every other weekend across the nordics. These events feed energy and passion into the community, but they are not the community. They are not âburnsâ. At least âAll-Inclusiveâ have the honesty to admit that they (probably) arenât. 

â

**Radical Inclusion** is maybe the most challenging of all of the 10 principles. It is **uncomfortable** (and should be uncomfortable) for each of us, and for the community as a whole. It is a challenge that reverberates through society, in conversations about equality, immigration, democracy and more. **By the hand of globalisation, it is truly a challenge of our time.** While I here try to look at systems and processes, inclusion is something deeply personal. We canât do it without starting with ourselves: with our own emotions, with our own home, our group of friends and the communities we live in.

So finally, I want to recognise the fact **I speak about inclusion from a point of privilege**. In fact, I believe that any voice speaking about the inclusion of someone other than themselves is a privileged voice. I want to ask all of you, as you speak about this topic, as you make decisions about inclusion, to recognise this privilege. **Your voice has the power to take away what some might consider their âhomeâ**, their very sense of belonging. I ask you to show humility, to show compassion.

I spent two weeks writing this text, and I have no intention of going into Facebook-debate-mode over it. If you want to start a thread on Talk, please invite me. If you want to contact me directly, please go ahead. I will personally not participate in the comments section of this post.

Thank you for reading,

/ Gustaf

label_values.label: Text
label_values.value:
LEDs ARE AWESOME group members, please take a moment to respond to this po!!

Until 3 weeks ago, facebook rules for "Public" facebook groups, such as LEDs ARE AWESOME, could effectively manage the membership and weed out fake accounts and spammers (about 10-20% of all membership requests). You had to be an approved member to post to this group, and keeping the high quality content of discussions here as it has been did not take a lot of moderator work and dilligence. And you all could be trusted to keep things civil and on-topic, which was awesome.

All that has changed under the new rules for public facebook groups. Either moderators must review many posts and a fair number of "visitor" comments for approval, or we open the floodgates to spammers who brazenly lie in response to the now-standard membership questions and immediately post their spam ads here. In just the last week, this has become a daily chore. I expect it will escalate quickly as other spammers catch on.

Our options as I see it are:
1. Keep LEDs ARE AWESOME facebook group "Public" as it has been, and have moderators constantly reviewing member posts and visitor comments, or mopping up the spam as it appears. As a Public facebook group, posts here are visible to everyone on facebook (and can be shared with nonmembers), as is the membership list of all 8,000 people in this group.
1. Change the status of LEDs ARE AWESOME facebook group from "Public" to "Private". This will hide all posts, and the membership list, from nonmembers. The existence of the group will still be "visible" on facebook, and anyone with a facebook account can still apply for membership, just as before. But also, if you try to share a post or crosspost from this group to anywhere else on acebook, the content won't be visible to nonmembers.

SO, I'm running a straw poll to help get a sense of what members who have made this AWESOME group what it is would prefer to do.

My preference would be to change LEDs ARE AWESOME to a "Private" group, as it appears to be more of a win than a loss if we want to keep the quality of the content here high without a lot of moderator intervention. There's more complicating the situation of "moderator approval" powers that I haven't gone into. But I may be overlooking something. If so, please let me know.

Please also feel free to discuss this decision in comments here.

Thank you for participating.

label_values.value:
I'm working to make this installation more rugged and need your advice.

This is "Water", from the exhibition "Emerging Sensation" by @[1487697168203149:274:Malin Bobeck Tadaa, Textile Artist].

This fall, it will go out on a nation wide tour as a part of an interactive ballet performance, with external technicians taking it a part and putting it back together every week and thousands of people interacting with it. How do I make sure it survive a 6 month tour?

The basic structure of the piece:
* Three sculptures, with only slight size differences.
* The largest one has about 300 pixels, divided in short strips. Each strip has either 24 pixels (arm sections) or 15 pixels (in the "flowers").
* Each piece has 3-4 touch sensors connected to interwoven silver coated thread, inside the "flowers".
* The installation should be controlled by ARTNET, being able to switch between different preprogrammed modes and colors.

My current plan:
* Teensy 4.1 with OctoWS2811 and Ethernet Kit. One for each sculpture.
I do not wish to gamble on Wifi, and i figured this was the best off-the-shelf solution for the combo of Ethernet, pixel control and extra pins for sensors.
* The controller package is made in 2 copies for each sculpture and easy to swap.
* WS2813 pixels in lengths of 27 and 15 pixels with 4 pin jst connectors. Each strip is manufactured to length by BTF Lighting (I don't trust my own soldering skills). I have about 2x the number of strips I need, for replacement.
* The stem is run on one channel, the 3 arms has one each. Optimally, i'd like to only feed them power from the bottom, but I might run an extra power cable to the other end if one of the longer (72 pixels) sections turn pink
* Adafruit AT42QT1010 boards for touch sensors, placed as close to the touch wires as possible.
* A 5V, 6A power supply available off the shelf at a big Swedish consumer electronics chain. To be able to get easy replacements and to know that whatever plugs into mains is properly CE marked.

Any questions?

Please hit me with your best ruggedness advice! 

Behind the scenes video from the original project here: https://vimeo.com/389787112
More photos and awesome art at Malin's website: http://www.malinbobeck.se

label_values.label: Text
label_values.value:
âEveryone engaged in politics is engaged in identity politics. This is not an insult, and itâs not controversial: we form and fold identities constantly, naturally. Identity is present in politics in the way gravity, evolution, or cognition is present in politics; that is to say, it is omnipresent in politics, because it is omnipresent in us.
There is no way to read the literature on how humans form and protect their personal and group identitiesâliterature I will survey in this bookâand believe any of us is immune. It runs so deep in our psyches, is activated so easily by even weak cues and distant threats, that it is impossible to speak seriously about how we engage with one another without discussing how our identities shape that engagement.â - Ezra Klein, âWhy Weâre Polarizedâ

The Nomination Committee for 2021, Gustaf Tadaa and Hugi Asgeirsson, have finished their work!

After surveying the board, we have found two people who would like to leave: Cindy Larsen and Jonas NÃ¤slund, while the other 5 would like to stay on for one more year. After an advice process on Talk, we decided to let all those who would like to stay on, do so.
As Jonas was the treasurer, we went looking for someone with âat least a little bit of experience with bookkeepingâ. We had three applications for this role: Hal Couzens, Christian Ubbesen and Stefano Mangiarotti. After interviewing all three, we have found them all exceptionally qualified for the job.
Since one of the main roles for the incoming treasurer would be to transition the role from an individual âTHE treasurerâ to a finance team, we invited all the three applicants to talk to each other to form that team. After having talked to each other, all three of them have agreed to step up as our brand new finance team (a.k.a. The Borderland Treshuras ð´ââ ï¸ ).
Since we formally need a treasurer on the board, the group decided to recommend Stefano Mangiarotti for that role, but also requested to have the other members be able to take his place.
For the second position on the board, we have decided to do a lottery.
Read more about why we did a lottery here: https://talk.theborderland.se/d/iJTt8dUI/electing-the-board-through-lotteri-
We had 53 people sign up for the lottery, out of which 38 were actual active members (as required by the bylaws). Gustaf assigned his daughter Iris to draw a name out of a hat and the winner was Malin Peterson!
Based on this, the nomination committee nominates the following people for the 2021 board:
Diana Monsberger (Chairperson)
Liselotte Norman
Olav Immanuel BÃ¸rjesson
Konrad Leffler
Mathias Gullbrandson
Stefano Mangiarotti (Treasurer)
Malin Peterson

Since one of the main roles for the incoming treasurer would be to transition the role from an individual âTHE treasurerâ to a finance team, we invited all the three applicants to talk to each other to form that team. After having talked to each other, all three of them have agreed to step up as our brand new finance team (a.k.a. The Borderland Treashuras ð´ââ ï¸ ).
Since we formally need a treasurer on the board, the group decided to recommend Stefano Mangiarotti for that role, but also requested to have the other members be able to take his place.
For the second position on the board, we have decided to do a lottery.
Read more about why we did a lottery here: https://talk.theborderland.se/d/iJTt8dUI/electing-the-board-through-lotteri-
We had 53 people sign up for the lottery, out of which 38 were actual active members (as required by the bylaws). Gustaf assigned his daughter Iris to draw a name out of a hat and the winner was Malin Peterson!
Based on this, the nomination committee nominates the following people for the 2021 board:
Diana Monsberger (Chairperson)
Liselotte Norman
Olav Immanuel BÃ¸rjesson
Konrad Leffler
Mathias Gullbrandson
Stefano Mangiarotti
Malin Peterson
